title: Vegan Chocolate Courgette Cake Recipe
summary: The chocolate courgette cake is a delightful vegan treat that cleverly balances rich cocoa with the subtle moisture of courgette. This combination creates a deeply chocolaty cake that remains moist and tender. The courgette, also known as zucchini, not only enhances the cake’s texture but also keeps it moist, making it an excellent choice for a plant-based dessert. The cake can be stored in an airtight container for up to 5 days, preserving its freshness. For longer storage, individual slices can be frozen, making it easy to enjoy a quick cake hit whenever desired. This recipe uses non-dairy milk and plant-based ingredients, perfect for those following a vegan diet. To prepare, you’ll need basic baking equipment like cake pans, parchment paper, and a mixing bowl. For best results, use high-quality cocoa powder and ensure all ingredients, such as self-raising flour and bicarbonate of soda, are well combined to achieve a smooth batter. Enjoy this delicious chocolate courgette cake as a satisfying dessert or a sweet snack!

method.description: Heat the oven to 160°C. Grease a 900g loaf tin with a little oil and line the base with baking parchment.
Whisk the sugar, cocoa powder, flour, bicarb and a big pinch of salt together in a large bowl until there are no lumps of sugar left.
Coarsely grate the courgette using a box grater (no need to peel).

Measure the oil, milk and vinegar into a jug. Whisk with a fork, then pour the wet ingredients into the dry. Add the courgette and chocolate chips and whisk everything together until a rich chocolate cake batter is formed.
Spoon the cake batter into your prepared loaf tin. Bake on the middle shelf in your oven for 50 mins-1 hr until a skewer inserted into the centre of the loaf comes out with a few dry crumbs attached.
Leave the loaf to cool in its tin before cutting into slices to serve.

chefs.name: Sophie Godwin
chefs.summary: Sophie Godwin is a cookbook author, food writer and chef. When she’s not catering weddings, she is rustling up accessible cookable bangers. She's helped us write multiple Mob cookbooks over the years and her recipes never disappoint. Her debut cookbook Sundays is out now.
